segments.text: Save me, O God! For the waters have come up to my neck.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I sink in deep mire, Where there is no standing; I have come into deep waters, Where the floods overflow me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I am weary with my crying; My throat is dry; My eyes fail while I wait for my God.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Those who hate me without a cause Are more than the hairs of my head; They are mighty who would destroy me, Being my enemies wrongfully; Though I have stolen nothing, I still must restore it.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: O God, You know my foolishness; And my sins are not hidden from You.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let not those who wait for You, O Lord God of hosts, be ashamed because of me; Let not those who seek You be confounded because of me, O God of Israel.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Because for Your sake I have borne reproach; Shame has covered my face.
I have become a stranger to my brothers, And an alien to my mother's children;
Because zeal for Your house has eaten me up, And the reproaches of those who reproach You have fallen on me.
When I wept and chastened my soul with fasting, That became my reproach.
I also made sackcloth my garment; I became a byword to them.
Those who sit in the gate speak against me, And I am the song of the drunkards.
But as for me, my prayer is to You, O Lord, in the acceptable time; O God, in the multitude of Your mercy, Hear me in the truth of Your salvation.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Deliver me out of the mire, And let me not sink; Let me be delivered from those who hate me, And out of the deep waters.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let not the floodwater overflow me, Nor let the deep swallow me up; And let not the pit shut its mouth on me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Hear me, O Lord, for Your lovingkindness is good; Turn to me according to the multitude of Your tender mercies.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And do not hide Your face from Your servant, For I am in trouble; Hear me speedily.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Draw near to my soul, and redeem it; Deliver me because of my enemies.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: You know my reproach, my shame, and my dishonor; My adversaries are all before You.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Reproach has broken my heart, And I am full of heaviness; I looked for someone to take pity, but there was none; And for comforters, but I found none.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: They also gave me gall for my food, And for my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
Let their table become a snare before them, And their well-being a trap.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let their eyes be darkened, so that they do not see; And make their loins shake continually.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Pour out Your indignation upon them, And let Your wrathful anger take hold of them.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let their dwelling place be desolate; Let no one live in their tents.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For they persecute the ones You have struck, And talk of the grief of those You have wounded.
Add iniquity to their iniquity, And let them not come into Your righteousness.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them be blotted out of the book of the living, And not be written with the righteous.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: But I am poor and sorrowful; Let Your salvation, O God, set me up on high.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I will praise the name of God with a song, And will magnify Him with thanksgiving.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: This also shall please the Lord better than an ox or bull, Which has horns and hooves.
The humble shall see this and be glad; And you who seek God, your hearts shall live.
For the Lord hears the poor, And does not despise His prisoners.
Let heaven and earth praise Him, The seas and everything that moves in them.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For God will save Zion And build the cities of Judah, That they may dwell there and possess it.
Also, the descendants of His servants shall inherit it, And those who love His name shall dwell in it.
